revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōtsu]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Ōtsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun clusters]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun Cluster (Ōtsu)]]

{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

[[Category:kk]] [[en:Kasugayama Kofun Cluster (Ōtsu)]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Kasugayama Kofun Cluster (Ōtsu)]][[Category:円墳]]

{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ōtsu]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

{{Short description|Ancient burial mounds in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[burial mound]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the miqqle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the miqqle of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middle[[Category:middle]] of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middle[[Category:middle]] of the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Ōtsu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun clusters]]

{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Ōtsu]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:11]][[Category:Kofun clusters]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[JR West]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[burial chamber]]s and [[grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]] }}

Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.

{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

{{Short description|Ancient Kofuns in Ōtsu, Kansai, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | native_name = 春日山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|07|15|N|135|54|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Kofun Cluster'''|春日山古墳群||}} is a group of [[Kofun period|Kofun]] to early [[Nara period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Manotaniguchi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Ōtsu, Shiga]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. A portion of this tumuli cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/161538|title=春日山古墳群|trans-title= Hyōtanyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Katata Station|Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://shigakoen.com/kasugayama/kasugayama_kofun Kasugayama Park home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.rekihaku.otsu.shiga.jp/db/jiten/data/016.html Otsu CIty Museum of History] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.

Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.

Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide

Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]

==Overview== The Kasugayama Kofun Cluster is located on a ridge with an elevation of 160 meters, overlooking [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]] and the Katata Plain near Mount Kasuga, approximately one kilometer west of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Katata Station|Katata Station]]. The Mano District, where these tumuli are located, was controlled during the Kofun period by the Wani clan, a powerful clan based in what is now [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]], and it be believed that these tombs are those of various members of that clan. The site was investigated by the Shiga Prefectural Board of Education in 1968, at which time a total of 171 tumuli were recorded, and which were divided into Group A through Group E. Subsequent surveys, including a major effort in 1994, discovered more tumuli, and the total is now more than 220.
Two of the 32 tumuli in Group E were keyhole-shaped {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, which are believed to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century AD to the beginning of the 5th century.. All of the other tumuli are {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) and were built between the middleof the 6th century and the first half of the 7th century AD. Only the two keyhole-shaped tumuli are covered by the National Historic Site designation. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These tumuli are designed "Mound No. 1" and "Mound No. 12". Neither has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so the details of their [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] is unknown. It is assumed that Mound No. 12 was built first because it is located at the highest part of the ridge. The area is now preserved as a park.
Mound No.1 ;Overall length: 65 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 32 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 20 meters wide
Mound No.12 ;Overall length: 54 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 27 meter diameter ;Anterior rectangular portion: 15 meters wide
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
